author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇava⋅kṛpā kata dine ha’be
upādhi⋅rahita⋅rati citte upajibe

kabe siddhadeha mora hoibe prakāśa
sakhī dekhāibe more jugala⋅vilāsa

dekhite dekhite rūpa, hoiba vātula
kadamba⋅kānane jā’ra tyaji’ jāti⋅kula

‘sveda’ ‘kampa’ ‘pulakāśru’ ‘vaivarṇya’ ‘pralaya’
‘stambha’ ‘svarabheda’ kabe hoibe udaya

bhāvamaya vṛndāvana heriba nayane
sakhīra kiṅkarī ha’ye seviba du’jane

kabe narottama⋅saha sākṣāt hoibe
kabe vā ‘prārthanā’⋅rasa citte praveśibe

caitanyadāsera dāsa chāḍi’ anya rati
karayuḍi’ māge āja śrī⋅caitanya mati
en_translation:
When will the mercy of my spiritual master and the devotees come? When will unalloyed love arise in my heart?

When will my perfected spiritual body manifest? When will a companion show me the divine pastimes of the Divine Couple?

Gazing upon their forms, I will become mad with love; in the kadamba forest, I will abandon all social conventions and family ties.

When will perspiration, trembling, thrills of ecstasy, tears, and fainting—along with stupefaction and a choking voice—manifest in me?

With my own eyes, I will behold the spiritual Vṛndāvana; becoming a maidservant of a companion, I will serve the Divine Couple.

When will I meet Narottama dāsa Ṭhākura? And when will the essence of his prayers enter my heart?

Abandoning all other attachments and becoming a servant of the servant of Caitanya, today, with folded hands, I beg for the spirit of Śrī Caitanya.
